hero_subtitle: SFTY structures multi-jurisdiction Travel Rule workflows by linking policy, case management and audit evidence to support VASP compliance with cross-border regulations.

problem_title: Cross-border Travel Rule workflows remain disconnected
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to translate varied jurisdiction-specific Travel Rule policies into consistent operational workflows. This gap creates delays in case handling and weakens the traceability of Travel Rule exceptions across borders.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects jurisdiction-specific Travel Rule requirements, provider outputs, exception cases and regulatory updates into one unified workflow for VASP compliance teams managing cross-border operations with clear traceability.
workflow_title: From Travel Rule exceptions to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY routes cross-border Travel Rule exceptions through review workflows to generate structured cases, preserve evidence, and prepare compliance reports across jurisdictions.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond Travel Rule. One compliance layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY transforms fragmented Travel Rule outputs into structured workflows, cases, decisions and audit-ready evidence across jurisdictions without replacing existing providers.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs translating jurisdictional Travel Rule policies into auditable workflows with provider orchestration, evidence preservation and enterprise-grade deployment control.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for cross-border Travel Rule?
faq_1_answer: SFTY structures cross-border Travel Rule workflows by connecting provider outputs, applying jurisdiction-specific policies, creating cases for high-risk events, and preserving audit-ready evidence for compliance review.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle Travel Rule exceptions?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes and documents Travel Rule exceptions in a connected workflow, supporting case creation for manual review and maintaining clear records of decision rationale and follow-up actions.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve Travel Rule evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves detailed evidence, including message data, case history, decision logs and provider outputs, to support audit-ready review and regulatory inspection readiness.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing Travel Rule providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY works above existing Travel Rule providers to connect routing, status tracking, exception handling and reporting without disrupting current compliance stacks.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace Travel Rule providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace Travel Rule providers but connects their outputs into structured workflows, cases and reports to enhance operational control and auditability.
final_description: Explore how SFTY connects jurisdictional policies, exception handling, case management and audit evidence for multi-jurisdiction Travel Rule compliance.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
